danger_signals.meaning: Lethal threat; collision results in immediate death/life loss

danger_signals.visual_form: Red spherical projectile descending the pyramid
Purple egg bouncing toward pyramid base, morphing into pursuing snake
danger_signals.meaning: Escalating threat level; transformation marks shift from passive to active pursuit

danger_signals.visual_form: Character approaching outer boundary of cube structure
danger_signals.meaning: Fatal fall risk; jumping off results in death

safety_signals.meaning: Temporary invulnerability; immobilizes all enemies for limited duration

safety_signals.visual_form: Floating colored discs positioned on either side of pyramid
safety_signals.meaning: Emergency teleport to pyramid top; safe passage if Coily pursues (he will fall to death)

safety_signals.visual_form: Cube shifts to designated goal color after landing
safety_signals.meaning: Progress toward level completion; safe interaction

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Controls cube coloration; moves diagonally through isometric space

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Coily: egg → snake transformation; Ugg/Wrongway: distinct flying patterns along pyramid edges
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Traverse pyramid structure in opposition to Q*bert's path; introduce chase mechanics

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Descend pyramid independently; non-lethal but obstructive
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Revert cube colors to original/former states, creating puzzle obstruction

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Single-pass cube coloration requirement; fewer enemies present
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemies introduced gradually; basic threat patterns

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Cubes require multiple touches to reach target color; color-cycling mechanics activate
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Increased enemy density and complexity; Slick/Sam actively revert progress

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Purple snake actively chases Q*bert down pyramid structure
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Threat escalates from passive to aggressive; player forced into evasive patterns

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Multicolored floating discs become interactive; pyramid resets to top
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Coily follows and falls to death; all enemies removed temporarily; bonus points awarded

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: All cubes reach target color simultaneously
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Progression to next stage; difficulty increase (additional cube-touch requirements)

environmental_storytelling.example: 28-cube pyramid serves as both game board and world boundary; physical limits reinforce danger of falling off edges
Purple = lethal (Coily, Ugg, Wrongway); Green = obstructive but harmless (Slick, Sam); Red ball = lethal projectile; Green ball = temporary safety

environmental_storytelling.technique: Enemy behavior patterns as environmental clues
environmental_storytelling.example: Coily's egg-to-snake transformation signals escalating threat; Ugg/Wrongway's Escher-like side traversal indicates unpredictable pathways; Slick/Sam's downward descent shows cube-reversion mechanics
Diagonal movement constraints and pyramid geometry impose puzzle logic; escape discs positioned symmetrically on sides as visual 'safe zones'

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Bounces passively toward pyramid bottom; no active threat
Pursues Q*bert actively; relentless chase until Q*bert escapes or uses disc
Follows Q*bert onto escape disc and falls to death, removing all active threats
Fly or hop along pyramid edges in Escher-like patterns from bottom corners toward top; leave when reaching edge
Descend pyramid; revert touched cubes to previous colors, undoing player progress
All enemies freeze temporarily; immobilization lasts limited duration before enemies resume
Instant death; no recovery or temporary effect

notes: Q*bert (NES 1989) uses a minimalist visual language where color and movement pattern are primary communicators of threat hierarchy. The isometric perspective creates pseudo-3D spatial boundaries that define the playable world and enforce puzzle constraints. No faction-based political conflict exists; instead, the game uses enemy archetype coloring (purple=lethal, green=obstructive) to communicate danger levels. World-state transitions are signaled primarily through enemy introduction sequences and cube-coloration requirement changes. The game lacks environmental narrative—danger and safety are entirely abstract and mechanical.
